[REASONING CONTEXT]
Amplify: systematic interval-by-interval mapping; verify supply/demand at each phase independently
Suppress: ad_hoc shortcuts; skipping cascade effects; misattributing capacity between intervals
[END REASONING CONTEXT]

## Solution

I'll map each time interval, tracking supply and demand for both cities.

### Cascade at t=0
- Substation A fails → 450MW demand shifts to tie line → only 200MW can flow
- Substation B sees 350+200 = 550MW > 400MW capacity → **trips on overload**
- **Both cities go completely dark at t=0**

### Time Intervals

| Interval | Duration | City A Supply | City A Unserved | City B Supply | City B Unserved | Total Unserved |
|---|---|---|---|---|---|---|
| t=0→10 | 10 min | 0 MW | 450 MW | 0 MW | 350 MW | 800 MW |
| t=10→15 | 5 min | 50 MW (gen) | 400 MW | 0 MW | 350 MW | 750 MW |
| t=15→30 | 15 min | 50 MW (gen) | 400 MW | 100 MW (gen) | 250 MW | 650 MW |
| t=30→120 | 90 min | 50+50 = 100 MW | 350 MW | 250+100 = 350 MW | 0 MW | 350 MW |

**t=30 detail:** Substation B restarts at 300MW. City B needs 350MW but has 100MW from generators, so only 250MW needed from Sub B. The remaining 50MW of Sub B capacity flows through the tie line (within 200MW limit) to City A.

### Unserved Energy Calculation

| Interval | Unserved Power × Time | MWh |
|---|---|---|
| t=0→10 | 800 × 10/60 | 133.33 |
| t=10→15 | 750 × 5/60 | 62.50 |
| t=15→30 | 650 × 15/60 | 162.50 |
| t=30→120 | 350 × 90/60 | 525.00 |

**Total = 133.33 + 62.50 + 162.50 + 525.00 = 883.33 MWh**

### **Answer: 883 MWh**
